key_insight: This paper introduces a Bayesian optimization method that searches for multiple distinct designs whose properties fall within a desired target range, rather than maximizing a single objective.
engineering_relevance: The approach is useful for materials and product design problems where acceptable property windows, cost, processability, and solution diversity matter more than one best-in-class answer.
method: Scores candidates by posterior probability of meeting a target range.
Extends naturally to parallel discovery of multiple distinct valid designs.
Uses range-aware acquisition to prioritize specification satisfaction over single-point optimization.
evidence_signal: Across benchmark tasks and two case studies, the method recovered larger and more diverse sets of valid designs than standard BO baselines and recent goal-seeking methods.
watch_item: Performance depends on how well the target range is specified and modeled; uncertain surrogate calibration could reduce reliability near window boundaries.
paper_title: Range-Aware Bayesian Optimization for Discovering Diverse Designs within Target Property Windows
